author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods
book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon XIX.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: If any persons who profess virginity shall disregard their profession, let them fulfil the term of digamists.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: And, moreover, we prohibit women who are virgins from living with men as sisters.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Whoever has professed virginity and afterwards annuls it, let him be cut off for four years.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: And virgins shall not go to any as to brothers.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: According to some of the ancient canons digamists were to be suspended from communion for one or two years, though Beveridge and others doubt whether the rule was not meant to apply to such marriages only as were contracted before a former one was dissolved.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Bingham thinks that it was intended to discountenance marrying after an unlawful divorce.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: The first part of this canon regards all young persons—men as well as women—who have taken a vow of virginity, and who, having thus, so to speak, betrothed themselves to God are guilty of a quasi digamy in violating that promise.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: They must therefore incur the punishment of digamy (successiva) which, according to St. Basil the Great, consisted of one year’s seclusion.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: This canon is found in Gratian’s Decretum (P.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: II., Causa xxvii., Q. i., c. xxiv.) as follows: “As many as have professed virginity and have broken their vow and contemned their profession shall be treated as digamists, that is as those who have contracted a second marriage.”
